_meta.source: CEV Informe Final — Tomo 'Hallazgos y recomendaciones' (2022)
_meta.structure: Each of 67 numbered recommendations carries an addressee and a horizon (corto/mediano/largo plazo). Monitored by a Comité de Seguimiento y Monitoreo. Estimated reparation need: COP 328.3 trillion (Contraloría, 2021 constant).
_meta.note: Encoded at block granularity with the key specific recommendations per block (faithful to the synthesis; not a verbatim transcription of every numbered article).

blocks.title: Avanzar en la construcción de paz como un proyecto nacional
recommendations: Full integral implementation of the Acuerdo Final de Paz (territorial, ethnic, gender approaches; accelerate the ethnic chapter and gender measures)
Security and reincorporation guarantees for FARC-EP ex-combatants (incl. widows/orphans of assassinated ex-combatants)
Create a Ministerio para la Paz y la Reconciliación
A new international policy, including restoring relations with Venezuela
Humanitarian measures and demining
Prioritize dialogue to end armed confrontation: a negotiated, definitive exit with the ELN (recover 2017-2018 advances)
A submission/dismantling policy for criminal and paramilitary-successor organizations via the Comisión Nacional de Garantías de Seguridad
Review and adjust rights-restoration and reintegration of de-linked children

blocks.title: Reparación integral, memoria, rehabilitación y reconocimiento de la dignidad de las víctimas
recommendations: State leadership of extrajudicial recognition-of-responsibility acts — incl. explicit State recognition in 'falsos positivos', in paramilitarism, and in the UP genocide, plus invisible victims (exiles, justice officials)
Non-regressive adjustment of the Victims & Land-Restitution Law and Ethnic Decrees toward integral, differentiated, transformative reparation
Recognition of exile as a grave violation with return/relocation measures
Recognition of victimized ex-combatants
Emergency compliance with international reparation orders
Strengthen integral physical/mental-health and psychosocial care
A national memory-and-truth policy incl. a permanent, government-independent Museo de Memoria de Colombia and a strengthened Human-Rights Archives strategy
A comprehensive forced-disappearance policy strengthening the UBPD for the 120,000+ disappeared, supporting the buscadoras

blocks.title: Consolidar una democracia incluyente, amplia y deliberativa
recommendations: A national political pact to deepen democracy and prevent political violence
Electoral/political reform (proportional inclusive representation, internal party democracy, financing transparency)
Citizen-participation and deliberative-dialogue mechanisms
A statute of rights/guarantees for social organizations and movements
Recognition and protection of social protest and mobilization as democratic participation — end its criminalization; graduated/proportional use of force
Public officials must cease stigmatizing human-rights defenders and leaders
Effective security guarantees for leaders, defenders and ex-combatants
Recognition and political participation of the campesinado as a subject of rights
Equality pacts and patriarchy-dismantling for women and LGBTIQ+
Concerted constitutional-rights guarantees for ethnic peoples

blocks.title: Enfrentar los impactos del narcotráfico y de la política de drogas
recommendations: A comprehensive shift FROM the drug war / prohibitionism TO strict legal regulation (regulación legal estricta): legalize cannabis (medicinal/recreational), decriminalize personal-use possession, treat drug use as public health, regulate coca cultivation with support for the campesino cocalero, end environmentally/community-harmful eradication
Reformulate international drug policy — advocate UN-level drug-policy reform and regional cooperation

blocks.title: Superar la impunidad, judicializar los entramados de criminalidad organizada y corrupción, mejorar el acceso a la justicia local
recommendations: Reform Fiscal General (Prosecutor General) selection for independence (merit, tenure, security)
Independent review of investigative methods
Prioritize investigation of systematic violence (DDHH/DIH) and large-scale corruption tied to organized crime
Protect judges, prosecutors, witnesses, victims
Prioritize investigation of third parties who interfere in judicial processes
Condition extradition on prior clarification of crimes and victims' rights in Colombia
An evidence-systematizing commission on organized-crime networks
Memorialize violence against the justice system
Improve local justice access for those facing poverty, remoteness, discrimination, disability, ethnic and gender barriers

blocks.title: Una nueva visión de seguridad para la paz
recommendations: Adopt a new security vision centered on HUMAN SECURITY (seguridad como bien público centrado en las personas) — overcome the war/enemy and drug-war paradigm
Transform the security sector: redefine military and police roles/missions; democratic civilian supervision; institutional-culture change (training in human rights, gender, community relations)
Strengthen preventive/penal/disciplinary/fiscal controls over the Military Forces, Police and intelligence
Guarantee classification/declassification and access to intelligence archives (Rec. 41)
Derogate the 30-year reserve, keeping a non-renewable 15-year maximum (Rec. 42)
Derogate Decree 2149 of 2017 with archive depuración and civil-society participation (Rec. 43); JEP to protect/preserve archives (Rec. 44)
Control private security companies under civilian oversight (Rec. 45)
Strengthen civilian firearms control (Rec. 46)
A differentiated rural and border security strategy (Rec. 47)
Subject international military-cooperation agreements to public debate and transparency (Rec. 48)

blocks.title: Contribuir a la paz territorial
recommendations: A sustainable territorial-development strategy reversing rural inequality
Decentralization and territorial autonomy with recognition of ethnic authorities
Complete the multipurpose cadastre (catastro multipropósito)
Agrarian reform to reverse extreme land concentration (smallholder access, progressive land taxation, recovery of unproductive/illicitly accumulated baldíos, regularization, environmental standards)
Define the agricultural frontier (frontera agrícola)
Constitute/expand/sanitize ethnic territories
Sustainable agri-food systems and food security
Ratify the Escazú Agreement
Reduce urban-rural gaps via the 16 PDET roadmaps and the 17 ethnic macro-territories; accelerate the Reforma Rural Integral
A comprehensive land-dispossession prevention/reversal policy with accelerated land restitution

blocks.title: Lograr una cultura para vivir en paz
recommendations: Education for peace (curricular inclusion of conflict history, human rights, non-violence, restorative coexistence)
Peace pacts in educational institutions
A coordinating instance under the new Peace Ministry joining culture, media and faith communities
Peace journalism and media self-analysis of war narratives
Faith communities to promote dignity, reconciliation and forgiveness
Know, disseminate and continue the Commission's legacy (the Informe, the Human-Rights Archive, the Sistema de Información Misional, the Transmedia Digital)
